methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Humanoid, a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Artem Sokolov, has raised $50M to date. The comparable universe spans the Robotics & Manufacturing sector, which includes 9 private peers (including 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics) and 1 public comparable (277810.KQ), though few align precisely on stage and valuation tier.

Given limited same-stage peer data, the Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable valuation tool. Using $50M in total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $125M. This method reflects observable step-ups in the robotics sector at comparable inflection points.

The Peer-Median approach was not applied due to insufficient granular comparables—while 12 same-sector companies exist, fewer than three meet tight filters for stage, valuation tier, and fundraising timing simultaneously. A broad-sector median would risk material misalignment with Humanoid's specific risk profile.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $106M (low) to $144M (high), with a median of $125M. Key uncertainties include the company's technical progress relative to peers, customer traction, and competitive positioning within an increasingly crowded robotics landscape. Single-method reliance warrants updating this assessment as additional comparable transactions or detailed operational metrics become available.
